title: Arlong’s Rule Tightens Around Nami
scene: On pages 16–19, the story moves through Arlong Park and Nami’s dangerous position among the fish-men. Usopp arrives ahead of the others and gets pulled into the fear surrounding Arlong’s domain.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: a huge monster...? so then those strange dents on the ground were...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Those're the tracks left behind by that thing! It's unimaginable huge!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: They said they brought it over from Grand Line.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: There're monsters there too!?

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: I... I even went to Arlong Park...!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong Pirates
dialogue.text: But some female member of the Arlong Pirates stopped me at the door! She was like a real witch!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: How dare she get in my way...! I wanna kill her 700...!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Then go ahead and die.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: PFFT!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: As long as you know you're going to end up dead, then feel free to fight!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Some excellent revenge that is!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: But let me just say this one thing!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Shanks to me,

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: and that witch lady at Arlong Park interfering,

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: You've narrowly escaped death twice!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Get out of my house once you're done with that tea.

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: I hate naive little brats like you!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Hey!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: He's just a little kid! Isn't that going a bit too far!?

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: So what if he's just a kid!? Anybody who wants to die a dog's death can go ahead and die for all I care!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: It's not like they have any desire to live on anyways!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: What!?

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: I know... of a CER- Tain child who chose to live a painful life far worse than death...

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Because she looks forward to a better future...!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: That's why I also- Lutely loathe brats like him,

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Who only think about rushing to their deaths!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: UU...

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: WHAT...

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: What am I supposed to do then...?

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: It's so... Trust- rating...!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: What about your mom...?

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: She's still alive.

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Then she must be worried.

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: So why don't you go home to your mom?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: You're actually a pretty good person,

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Even though you do have a tattoo.

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Well isn't that prejudiced of you!? Though I can't say I know much about you...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Ah that's right. My name's Capt. Usoppp.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I came here looking for Nami but...

summary: This part builds the Arlong Park conflict by showing how Nami’s choices look contradictory from outside while Arlong’s power keeps the island trapped.
